title: QoreDB plugin registry index
description: Catalogue (machine-readable) of all approved plugins in the QoreDB marketplace. Generated from the per-plugin manifests under plugins/<id>/<version>/plugin.json. Powers the marketplace UI on https://qoredb.com/marketplace and the Marketplace tab inside the QoreDB app.

registryVersion.description: Schema version of this index. Bumped only when the index shape changes in a way that breaks consumers.

generatedAt.description: When the index was last regenerated from the on-disk manifests.

id.description: Reverse-DNS plugin id, e.g. `qoredb.danger-guard`. Must match `plugin.json#id`.

category.description: Marketplace browsing category. Mirrors `plugin.json#category` of the latest version.

latestVersion.description: Convenience pointer to the most recent entry of `versions`.

kind.description: `executable` means the plugin ships a sandboxed WASM module (manifest has a `runtime` block). `declarative` means the plugin contributes static data only.

category.description: Marketplace browsing category copied from this version's `plugin.json#category`.

abiVersion.description: Host ABI version the plugin was built against. Current: 1.

capabilities.description: Flat list of capabilities requested by the manifest. Surfaced as badges in the UI; the canonical structured shape lives in the manifest.

integrity.description: sha256 of the WASM module bytes. Plugins without integrity are surfaced as `Unsigned` in the QoreDB plugins panel.

contributes.description: Summary of declarative contributions, to power the marketplace cards without downloading the archive.

commands.description: Bare command ids the plugin exposes.

url.description: Canonical download URL of `plugin.zip` (manifest + .wasm bundled at the archive root).

sha256.description: sha256 of the archive bytes. The QoreDB app verifies this end-to-end before the manifest is parsed.

manifestUrl.description: Canonical URL of the plugin.json — handy for editors and audit tools that want the manifest without unpacking the archive.
